content.subtitle: Use
rows: To express a wish about the present or a general situation
Maria wishes she **lived** near the sea.
I wish I **wasn't** so nervous before exams.
To express regret about something that happened in the past
He wishes he **had listened** to his teacher's advice.
I wish we **hadn't stayed** out so late last night.
To express annoyance about someone's behaviour, or to complain
I wish the neighbours **would stop** making so much noise.
I wish you **wouldn't leave** your things everywhere.
To express wishes about what is possible now or in the future
She wishes she **could play** the piano.
I wish I **could stay** longer, but I have to leave.

content.subtitle: Extra notes
items: With wish + past simple, both was and were are acceptable for I/he/she/it. Were is the more formal option: I wish I was/were a better cook.
If only can replace wish with the same meaning: If only I was/were a better cook.

content.subtitle: Be careful
items: We don't use would when wishing for something about ourselves: I wish I knew the answer. (not I wish I would know the answer.)
For situations that might really happen in the future, use hope instead of wish: I hope the weather is nice tomorrow.

sections.title: A: Complete with the correct form
sections.instructions: Complete each sentence using wish and the verb in brackets.

items.sentence: I wish I ...... (know) how to cook better.
Ella wishes she ...... (not be) afraid of spiders.
We wish we ...... (can) travel around the world.
He wishes he ...... (take) more photos on holiday.
I wish my brother ...... (not leave) his clothes on the floor.
Sophie wishes she ...... (not lose) her keys yesterday.
I wish I ...... (be) more confident.
Daniel wishes he ...... (can play) the guitar.

sections.title: B: Choose the correct option
sections.instructions: Choose the best option for each sentence.

items.sentence: I wish I **understand / understood / had understood** maths better.
She wishes she **wouldn't be / weren't / hadn't been** so busy every weekend.
I wish I **can / could / had been able** ski.
I wish you **don't leave / would stop / had stopped** making that noise.
They wish they **visited / had visited / would visit** the museum yesterday.
If only I **am / were / had been** more organised.

sections.title: C: Rewrite with wish or if only
sections.instructions: Rewrite each sentence so it keeps the same meaning.

items.sentence: I don't know how to dance. → ......
items.answer: I wish I knew how to dance.

items.sentence: My sister always borrows my clothes. → ......
items.answer: I wish my sister wouldn't borrow my clothes.

items.sentence: I forgot my homework yesterday. → ......
items.answer: I wish I had remembered my homework yesterday.

items.sentence: I can't drive a car. → ......
items.answer: I wish I could drive a car.

items.sentence: It may be sunny tomorrow (possible). → ......
I'm not famous. → ......
items.answer: If only I was famous./If only I were famous.

sections.title: D: Error correction
sections.instructions: Each sentence has one mistake. Write the correct sentence.

items.sentence: I wish I would know the answer.
items.answer: I wish I knew the answer.

items.sentence: He wishes he would be stronger.
items.answer: He wishes he was stronger./He wishes he were stronger.

items.sentence: I wish you will be quieter.
items.answer: I wish you would be quieter.

items.sentence: She wishes she went to bed earlier last night.
items.answer: She wishes she had gone to bed earlier last night.

items.sentence: I wish I could won the competition.
items.answer: I wish I had won the competition.

items.sentence: I wish the test is easy tomorrow.
items.answer: I hope the test is easy tomorrow.

sections.title: E: Key word transformation
sections.instructions: Complete the second sentence so it has a similar meaning to the first. Use the word given.

items.context: I don't have enough money to buy a laptop.
items.sentence: I wish I ...... enough money to buy a laptop. **HAD**

items.context: It's a pity he didn't practise more before the match.
items.sentence: He wishes he ...... more before the match. **PRACTISED**

items.context: I'm bad at remembering names and I regret it.
items.sentence: I wish I ...... at remembering names. **WERE**

items.context: The dog barks all night and it annoys me.
items.sentence: I wish the dog ...... all night. **BARK**

items.context: I can't play chess and I want to.
items.sentence: If only I ...... play chess. **COULD**

items.context: We didn't save our work and now it's gone.
items.sentence: We wish we ...... our work earlier. **SAVED**
